source: Usd Optimize operation catalog; single source of truth for op existence, routing metadata (manifest fields), and this-repo curation (status/rationale/wired_into). Replaces the former manifest.json + _curation.json + per-op <key>.md split.

operations.title: Box Clip
operations.summary: Box Clip removes or retains geometry based on an axis-aligned bounding box (AABB) region.

operations.title: Compute Extents
operations.summary: Compute Extents calculates and authors the `extent` attribute for meshes.

operations.title: Count Vertices
operations.summary: Count Vertices is a hidden analysis utility that categorizes meshes by vertex count into high, very high, and extreme buckets.

operations.title: Decimate Meshes
operations.summary: Decimate Meshes reduces polygon count while preserving mesh shape as much as possible.

parameter_prerequisites.canonical_question: What's the smallest surface detail (in mm) you need to preserve?

recommendation_signals.signal: High-density outlier meshes detected — meshes with triangle density disproportionate to their physical extent are strong candidates for decimation. This Phase-2c validator reads actual geometry and is the canonical over-tessellation signal (SA does not flag density; it reads no geometry arrays).

recommendation_signals.note: maxMeanError is inherently scale-aware: over-tessellated meshes (e.g. a 1M-poly screw at 20mm) lose most triangles because nearly all vertices fall within the error budget. Under-tessellated meshes barely change. No per-mesh targeting is needed — apply uniformly to all meshes.

anti_patterns: Do not frame as 'reduce by X%'. Rate-mode bypasses the silhouette-preserving error budget.
Do not ask which meshes to target. maxMeanError handles density differences automatically.
Do not offer triangle-count or percentage options unless the user explicitly provides a rate-based constraint.

operations.title: De-duplicate Geometry
operations.summary: De-duplicate Geometry finds meshes that are geometrically identical (or near-identical) and replaces duplicates with instances of a single prototype.

operations.title: De-duplicate Hierarchies
operations.summary: De-duplicate Hierarchies identifies structurally-identical sub-hierarchies and collapses them into shared prototypes with instanceable references at each original site.
operations.notes: Operates at the subtree level: collapses structurally-identical sub-hierarchies into shared prototypes referenced from each original site (referencing prims marked instanceable=true). Contrast deduplicateGeometry, which operates on individual mesh data. ADVISORY-ONLY for deep mid-level reuse: does not consolidate deeply-nested component/subcomponent reuse — author shared prototypes directly for that case; use this operator only to suggest candidates. The bounded recursive descent authors mid-level subcomponent sharing DIRECTLY via the value-hash nested-library rewrite (restructure-decision deduplicate-internally branch); use deduplicateHierarchies / organizePrototypes only as a CANDIDATE SUGGESTER, never as the authoring mechanism for mid-level reuse. deduplicateGeometry remains the last-mile leaf cleanup.

operations.title: Delete Hidden Prims
operations.summary: Delete Hidden Prims finds and deletes all prims that have their visibility set to `invisible`.

operations.title: Delete Prims
operations.summary: Delete Prims is a hidden utility operation that permanently removes specified prims from the stage's edit target layer.

operations.title: Dice Meshes
operations.summary: Dice Meshes cuts meshes into smaller pieces along a 3D grid — like slicing a block of cheese with a wire grid.

operations.title: Edit Stage Metrics
operations.summary: Edit Stage Metrics modifies a stage's global metrics — up axis and linear units.

operations.title: Find Coinciding Geometry
operations.summary: Find Coinciding Geometry detects meshes that occupy the same space — overlapping or near-identical geometry that causes z-fighting and wasted rendering.

operations.title: Find Flat Hierarchies
operations.summary: Find Flat Hierarchies identifies prims with an excessively large number of children — "flat" hierarchy patterns where a single prim has hundreds or thousands of direct children.

operations.title: Find Occluded Meshes
operations.summary: Find Occluded Meshes detects geometry that is completely hidden inside other geometry and therefore never visible.

ordering.rationale: Remove internal geometry before spending compute on meshCleanup, deduplicateGeometry, decimation, or any other op. Dead weight is removed first.

scoping.trigger: SA validation_scope.cross_component_pairs not explicitly transparent (enclosure_opaque true or unset)
scoping.exclude: Pairs where the enclosing geometry has a transparent/translucent material (opacity < 1.0, transmission shader, glass MDL, alpha-blend mode). Objects visible through transparent enclosures must NOT be removed.
scoping.asset_types: Equipment, machines, vehicles, cabinets, housings, enclosures, pumps, motors, compressors, sealed assemblies — anything with an opaque shell/casing that could hide internal parts.

fields.source: SA validation_scope.cross_component_pairs not explicitly transparent (enclosure_opaque true or unset)
fields.description: Enclosing/enclosed boundary-ID pairs from SA §2.1, derived from boundary nomination (candidate_source hash OR semantics) and resolved to prim paths via asset_boundary_suggestions.boundaries[]. Pairs explicitly marked transparent (enclosure_opaque: false) are excluded; true or unset qualify (unknown opacity is resolved by the probe). Without scoped pairs, findOccludedMeshes has no scope and must not run on the full stage.

elicit_from_user.canonical_question: These enclosed assets contain internal geometry invisible from outside. Remove the occluded prims found by the scoped probe? (you lose the internals)
elicit_from_user.context: Present per asset on the Phase 7 iteration-2 opt-in menu, with the occluded-prim count and saving from the already-run scoped probe.

action_chain.pattern: Run findOccludedMeshes in analysis mode on the scoped pairs in Phase 4 (scoped Tier 3 probe, no approval). It produces a list of fully-occluded prim paths. Feed those paths to removePrims; only the deletion step is intent-gated (Phase 7 iteration-2 opt-in).

apply_approval.detection: Scoped probe runs in Phase 4 without approval (bounded by scope + timeout_recorded).
apply_approval.deletion: Removing the discovered occluded prims is intent-gated — offered per asset on the Phase 7 iteration-2 opt-in menu.

operations.title: Find Overlapping Meshes
operations.summary: Find Overlapping Meshes detects interfering geometry — meshes whose surfaces intersect or penetrate each other.

operations.title: Fit Primitives
operations.summary: Fit Primitives analyzes meshes and replaces them with simpler geometric primitives (spheres, cylinders, cones, cubes) when the mesh closely matches one of those shapes.

operations.title: Flatten Hierarchy
operations.summary: Flatten Hierarchy removes redundant Xform prims from a stage's hierarchy, reducing prim count.

operations.title: generateAtlasUVs
operations.summary: Auto UV Unwrap generates texture coordinates (UVs) by unfolding mesh surfaces into 2D.

operations.title: Generate Normals
operations.summary: Generate Normals computes and authors vertex normals for meshes.

operations.title: Generate Projection UVs
operations.summary: Generate Projection UVs creates texture coordinates by projecting them onto meshes using one of several projection methods (planar, cylindrical, spherical, cubic, or triplanar).

operations.title: Generate Scene
operations.summary: Generate Scene creates synthetic test scenes by procedurally placing meshes in a layout.

operations.title: Manifold Meshes
operations.summary: **Legacy command — use `meshCleanup` with `makeManifold: true` instead.** This operation exists for backward compatibility.

curation.rationale: deprecated: meshCleanup: legacy hole-closing command superseded by meshCleanup with makeManifold:true.

operations.title: Merge Static Meshes
operations.summary: Merge Static Meshes combines multiple meshes that share common properties into single merged meshes.

curation.notes: First-class but intent-gated Phase-4 step that EXECUTES the manifest `merge` disposition (usd-structure-assessment disposition matrix). Runs WITHIN a prototype only (merge once, benefit N instances), never across an instance boundary; ordered after occluded-geometry removal and ahead of the meshCleanup -> deduplicateGeometry -> computeExtents tail. Fails closed on strong-identity units (only weak/none identity may be fused) and on bounds-incoherent fusions (hierarchy-dedupe-rewrite-tool-spec.md §9 merge-eligibility guard). Credited as an axis-B prim-count / scene-graph reduction (traversal + stage-open + memory + draw calls), NEVER as a disk win; a conditional vertex-weld tail credits reclaimed bytes on axis C via disk_win_source=vertex_weld.

operations.title: Merge Vertices
operations.summary: **Legacy command — use `meshCleanup` instead.** This operation exists for backward compatibility.

curation.rationale: deprecated: meshCleanup: legacy standalone vertex welder superseded by meshCleanup.

operations.title: Mesh Cleanup
operations.summary: Mesh Cleanup performs a suite of mesh repair operations to fix common topological defects.

operations.title: Optimize Materials
operations.summary: Optimize Materials reduces the number of materials in a scene by deduplicating identical materials and consolidating similar ones.

operations.title: Optimize Primvars
operations.summary: Optimize Primvars reduces memory usage by optimizing how primvar (per-vertex/per-face attributes like UVs, colors) data is stored.

operations.title: Optimize Skeleton Roots
operations.summary: Optimize Skeleton Roots merges all skinned meshes within each UsdSkelRoot to improve GPU skinning performance.

operations.title: Optimize Time Samples
operations.summary: Optimize Time Samples removes redundant time samples from animated attributes.

operations.title: Organize Prototypes
operations.summary: Organize Prototypes moves internal scene-graph instance prototypes under a user-specified namespace (class prim).

operations.title: Compute Pivot
operations.summary: Compute Pivot recalculates and sets pivot points (transform origins) for meshes or transforms.

operations.title: Primitives to Meshes
operations.summary: Primitives to Meshes converts USD geometric primitives (UsdGeomSphere, UsdGeomCylinder, UsdGeomCone, UsdGeomCube) into polygon mesh representations.

curation.rationale: specialty: escape-hatch — the only path to convert analytic primitives back to UsdGeomMesh for downstream tools that reject primitives (some renderers, physics, exporters). Recommend only when the downstream context explicitly requires mesh output.

operations.title: Print Stats
operations.summary: Print Stats is a hidden diagnostic operation that outputs scene statistics including prim counts, mesh counts, vertex/face totals, and optionally primvar and timing information.

operations.title: Prune Leaves
operations.summary: Prune Leaves finds and removes leaf grouping primitives — Xforms and Scopes that contain no meaningful children (or only other empty groups).

operations.title: Python Script
operations.summary: Python Script executes a user-defined Python script as a Usd Optimize operation.

curation.rationale: specialty: escape-hatch op used by usd-optimize-create-proxy's USD-authoring recipes; not part of the general optimization flow.

operations.title: Remesh Meshes
operations.summary: Remesh Meshes regenerates mesh topology to create a more uniform triangle distribution while preserving the original shape.

operations.title: Remove Attributes
operations.summary: Remove Attributes removes or blocks specified attributes from prims.

operations.title: Remove Prims
operations.summary: Remove Prims identifies and removes invisible prims and orphaned overs from a USD stage.

operations.title: Remove Small Geometry
operations.summary: Remove Small Geometry finds and removes meshes that are below a size threshold.

operations.title: Remove Untyped Prims
operations.summary: Remove Untyped Prims deletes prims that have no USD schema type.

operations.title: Remove Unused UVs
operations.summary: Remove Unused UVs finds and removes texture coordinate (UV) attributes that are not referenced by any bound material.

operations.title: RTX Mesh Count
operations.summary: RTX Mesh Count is a hidden analysis operation that counts the number of RTX acceleration structures, RTX meshes, and unique RTX meshes in the scene.

operations.title: Shrinkwrap
operations.summary: Shrinkwrap converts a polygon soup into a bounding watertight mesh, with controllable mechanisms to generate loose and tight surface proxies.

operations.title: Sparse Meshes
operations.summary: Sparse Meshes reduces meshes with poor spatial density — geometry that occupies a large bounding box relative to its actual surface area.

curation.rationale: specialty: bespoke triage — surfaced from usd-validation-runner Tier 2 sparse-mesh findings; reach for it on user request, not from a named pipeline.

operations.title: Split Meshes
operations.summary: Split Meshes breaks meshes into smaller pieces based on geometric connectivity or spatial clustering.

operations.title: Subdivide Meshes
operations.summary: Subdivide Meshes increases mesh polygon density by subdividing faces.

operations.title: Triangulate Meshes
operations.summary: Triangulate Meshes converts all polygon faces to triangles.

operations.title: Utility Function
operations.summary: Utility Function is a container for small one-off operations that don't merit their own plugin.

curation.rationale: specialty: bespoke ad-hoc SO scripting (Deinstance / Unbind Materials / Set Instanceable / Flatten Instances); recommend only when a recipe needs one of those structural sub-functions.
